title: Kanpur Memorial Church
tagline: Kanpur Memorial Church — spiritual town of Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: The Kanpur Memorial Church, also known as the All Souls' Church, is a church located in Kanpur, India that belongs to the Church of North India, a united Protestant denomination.
overview.description: The Kanpur Memorial Church, also known as the All Souls' Church, is a church located in Kanpur, India that belongs to the Church of North India, a united Protestant denomination. It was completed and consecrated in 1876 to commemorate fallen British soldiers during the Siege of Cawnpore in 1857. An initial proposal to build a church in Kanpur came from the United Society Partners in the Gospel.

topPlaces.name: Kanpur Memorial Well
topPlaces.description: The Kanpur Memorial Well, formerly known as the Cawnpore Memorial and the Bibighar Memorial, is a monument located within the grounds of the Memorial Church within the military cantonment of Kanpur, Uttar Pradesh, India. It was completed in 1863 in remembrance of the British women and children who were buried in the Kanpur well following the Siege of Cawnpore during the Indian Rebellion of 1857.

topPlaces.name: Kanpur Cantonment
topPlaces.description: Kanpur Cantonment (popularly referred to as Kanpur Cantt) is a military cantonment in Kanpur, Uttar Pradesh, India. The Kanpur Cantonment was established in the year 1811 and is situated on the right bank of river Ganges and is bound by Kanpur city area on the remaining three sides.

topPlaces.name: Massacre Ghat
topPlaces.description: Satti Chaura Ghat (Satī Caurā Ghāţ) or Massacre Ghat is a famous ghat in Kanpur, the industrial hub of Uttar Pradesh state in north India. It is located on the bank of the River Ganges in Kanpur Cantonment near Jajmau.

topPlaces.name: Kanpur Sangrahalaya
topPlaces.description: Kanpur Sangrahalaya (Hindi: कानपुर पुरातत्व संग्रहालय), also known as Kanpur Museum, is the official museum in Kanpur, the industrial hub of Uttar Pradesh, India. It was built by the British as King Edward Memorial Hall in honour of Edward VII. It is a storehouse of artifacts and exhibits that tell the story of the events and people who have influenced the making of Kanpur city, and tell many interesting details about the historical past of the city.

topPlaces.name: Phool Bagh
topPlaces.description: Ganesh Shankar Vidyarthi Udyan / Phool Bagh (Hindi: गणेश शंकर विद्यार्थी उद्यान / फूल बाग़, कानपुर ), earlier Queen's Park, is an urban city park in Kanpur, the industrial hub of Uttar Pradesh in North India. It has been the location for many public meetings and political rallies in Kanpur from the early twentieth century.

topPlaces.name: Jajmau
topPlaces.description: Jajmau also known as Jajesmow during British Era, is a suburb of Kanpur, India. It is situated on the banks of the Ganges River, and is an industrial suburb.

topPlaces.name: Nana Rao Park
topPlaces.description: Nana Rao Park / Company Bagh (Hindi: नाना राव पार्क / कम्पनी बाग़, कानपुर ) is a public city park in Kanpur, the industrial hub of Uttar Pradesh, India, built after Indian independence in honor of Nana Sahib. Prior to Indian Independence the location was known as Memorial Well and commemorated the massacre of British women and children during the Indian Rebellion of 1857.

topPlaces.name: Z Square Mall, Kanpur
topPlaces.description: Z Square Mall is a shopping mall cum entertainment complex in the Indian city of Kanpur. The mall is spread on an area of five acres in the middle of Kanpur city with 84,000 square metres of built-up area and the presence of more than 150 National & International brands.

items.note: The Kanpur Memorial Well, formerly known as the Cawnpore Memorial and the Bibighar Memorial, is a monument located within the grounds of the Memorial Church within the military cantonment of Kanpur, Uttar Pradesh, India. It was completed in 1863 in remembrance of the British women and children who were buried in the Kanpur well following the Siege of Cawnpore during the Indian Rebellion of 1857.

Kanpur Cantonment (popularly referred to as Kanpur Cantt) is a military cantonment in Kanpur, Uttar Pradesh, India. The Kanpur Cantonment was established in the year 1811 and is situated on the right bank of river Ganges and is bound by Kanpur city area on the remaining three sides.

Satti Chaura Ghat (Satī Caurā Ghāţ) or Massacre Ghat is a famous ghat in Kanpur, the industrial hub of Uttar Pradesh state in north India. It is located on the bank of the River Ganges in Kanpur Cantonment near Jajmau.

Kanpur Sangrahalaya (Hindi: कानपुर पुरातत्व संग्रहालय), also known as Kanpur Museum, is the official museum in Kanpur, the industrial hub of Uttar Pradesh, India. It was built by the British as King Edward Memorial Hall in honour of Edward VII. It is a storehouse of artifacts and exhibits that tell the story of the events and people who have influenced the making of Kanpur city, and tell many interesting details about the historical past of the city.

Ganesh Shankar Vidyarthi Udyan / Phool Bagh (Hindi: गणेश शंकर विद्यार्थी उद्यान / फूल बाग़, कानपुर ), earlier Queen's Park, is an urban city park in Kanpur, the industrial hub of Uttar Pradesh in North India. It has been the location for many public meetings and political rallies in Kanpur from the early twentieth century.
Jajmau also known as Jajesmow during British Era, is a suburb of Kanpur, India. It is situated on the banks of the Ganges River, and is an industrial suburb.

Nana Rao Park / Company Bagh (Hindi: नाना राव पार्क / कम्पनी बाग़, कानपुर ) is a public city park in Kanpur, the industrial hub of Uttar Pradesh, India, built after Indian independence in honor of Nana Sahib. Prior to Indian Independence the location was known as Memorial Well and commemorated the massacre of British women and children during the Indian Rebellion of 1857.

Z Square Mall is a shopping mall cum entertainment complex in the Indian city of Kanpur. The mall is spread on an area of five acres in the middle of Kanpur city with 84,000 square metres of built-up area and the presence of more than 150 National & International brands.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kanpur Memorial Church?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kanpur Memorial Church is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kanpur Memorial Church?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kanpur Airport (1 km) and the nearest railway station is Kanpur Central (2 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kanpur Memorial Church?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kanpur Memorial Church at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kanpur Memorial Church?
faq.a: Stays in Kanpur Memorial Church start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kanpur Memorial Church?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kanpur Memorial Well, Kanpur Cantonment, Massacre Ghat, Kanpur Sangrahalaya, Phool Bagh.
